description: 3.- 19-12-2024 - Email from Michael Roberts (Assurance Deputy Director, CO) to Vincent Devine (Government Chief Security Officer, CO)
text:
Director, CO) to Vincent Devine (Government Chief Security Officer, CO)

- Forwarded message ---------

From: Michael Roberts Personal
Date: Thu, 19 Dec 2024 at 13:54

Subject: Fwd: NSV and HoL

To: Vincent Devine Personal
weceeec nes Forwarded message ---------
From: Gerard McGurk Personal

Date: Thu, 19 Dec 2024 at 09:53
Subject: RE: NSV and HoL

To: Vita Maynard Personal Michael Roberts
Personal Personal
Cc: Tom Bramley Personal
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Thanks Vita. This is very helpful confirmation of what we [FCDO] understood the
position to be. | will keep you updated as and when | receive a name.

As | understand it, our Perm Sec is considering whether to proceed with seeking
agrement for the Head of Mission role alongside the security clearance process. If/if
the nominee is a member of the HoL, then it does make the process less bumpy.
Assuming the Government in question accept the nomination.

Gerard

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

To: Michael Roberts 
Cc: Gerard McGurk 
; Tom Bramley 
Subject: Re: NSV and HoL
Hi Gerard, 
No vetting (as in SC/DV), but 
 reserve the right to clear for STRAP (i.e. 
). 
Vita
On Wed, 18 Dec 2024 at 18:58, Michael Roberts 
wrote:
Gerard, good evening again,
You asked earlier today whether an individual from the House of Lords needs to go 
through vetting. 
From a recap of the paper at GSB in October, such an individual would be exempt. Tom and 
Vita will be best placed to answer further policy questions (and I must confess to never 
knowing how STRAP works at the best of times, never mind in this scenario!).
best,
Michael
-- 
Michael Roberts
Assurance Deputy Director
UKSV, Government Security Group
Cabinet Office
E:
M:
NSV Privacy Notice
From: Vita Maynard 
Sent: 19 December 2024 09:30

parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
